serving.natural_language_portion: One standard plate of Schezwan Vegetable Noodles weighs approximately 400 g, providing around 550 kcal per serving.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Noodles, Chinese, chow mein, dry and Stir-fried noodles with vegetables (SR Legacy / Foundation Foods); supplemented by Singapore HPB Foodo nutrient composition data for stir-fried noodle dishes
nutrient_source.organization: U.S. Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service; Health Promotion Board, Singapore
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Schezwan Vegetable Noodles exists in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB Foodo. Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy entries for stir-fried noodles with vegetables (FDC ID 172030 and related entries) and HPB Singapore data for fried noodle dishes, adjusted for the Schezwan sauce component (higher sodium, modest added sugar, chilli oil). Input record values (energy 137.5 kcal, protein 3.0 g, fat 5.0 g, carbohydrate 18.75 g, sodium 300 mg) are consistent with this adapted range and are retained. Cholesterol is 0.0 mg (confirmed: no animal-derived ingredients). Monounsaturated and polyunsaturated fat data not available at this level of preparation detail; set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; supplemented by University of Sydney GI Database entry for wheat noodles / stir-fried noodle dishes
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Schezwan Vegetable Noodles. GI of 58 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for wheat-based stir-fried noodles (GI range 50–65 depending on cooking time and sauce composition). A midpoint of 58 is used, consistent with Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283) for boiled then stir-fried wheat noodles. Glycemic load per serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (58 × 75.0) / 100 = 43.5 (carbohydrate per 400 g serving = 18.75 × 4 = 75.0 g).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 300 mg/100 g; 1200 mg per standard 400 g serving
hypertension.guidance: A single 400 g serving delivers approximately 1200 mg sodium — 52% of the WHO 2300 mg daily limit — primarily from Schezwan sauce and soy sauce; patients with hypertension should request reduced sauce, avoid additional salt, and limit portion size to 200 g or less.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined wheat noodles with GI ~58 and glycemic load 43.5 per 400 g serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: The high glycemic load of 43.5 per standard serving (driven by refined wheat noodles and added sugar in Schezwan sauce) poses significant postprandial glucose risk; patients with Type 2 Diabetes should substitute whole-wheat or konjac noodles, request reduced-sugar sauce, and limit portion to half a plate (200 g).

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sodium 1200 mg per serving; saturated fat low at 0.75 g/100 g; trans fat trace at 0.03 g/100 g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: While saturated fat is low (3 g per 400 g serving) and cholesterol is zero, the high sodium load of approximately 1200 mg per serving is a meaningful cardiovascular risk factor; patients with CVD should request reduced-sauce preparation and pair with a potassium-rich side dish to offset sodium.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Nightshade ingredients (bell peppers, green chillies, chilli in Schezwan sauce) may aggravate solanine-sensitive RA
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: RA patients with nightshade sensitivity should request substitution of bell peppers and chillies with non-nightshade vegetables such as cabbage and carrots, and choose whole-wheat noodles to reduce glycaemic-load-driven inflammation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Schezwan Vegetable Noodles (also spelled Szechuan or Sichuan Veg Noodles) is a popular Indo-Chinese stir-fried noodle dish widely available in Singapore hawker centres, food courts, and delivery platforms. The dish consists of boiled wheat noodles tossed in a wok over high heat with a spicy, tangy Schezwan sauce made from dried red chillies, garlic, ginger, soy sauce, and vinegar, combined with stir-fried vegetables including cabbage, carrots, bell peppers, onions, and green chillies. The result is a smoky, spicy, slightly sour noodle dish with a chewy texture and vibrant colour. Per 100 g, it provides approximately 137.5 kcal, 3.0 g protein, 5.0 g fat (0.75 g saturated), 18.75 g carbohydrate, 2.0 g sugar, 1.5 g dietary fibre, and 300 mg sodium. The glycemic index is estimated at 58 (medium), yielding a high glycemic load of 43.5 per standard 400 g plate, making portion control important for individuals managing blood glucose. Sodium content is borderline at 300 mg/100 g (1200 mg per serving), primarily from Schezwan sauce and soy sauce, which is a concern for hypertension and cardiovascular disease. The dish is vegetarian, vegan, and halal-suitable. It contains gluten (wheat noodles) and soy allergens. Clinically, it is best consumed in reduced portions with whole-wheat or konjac noodle substitutions for diabetic and weight-conscious individuals.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Schezwan Vegetable Noodles exists in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB Foodo. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy stir-fried noodle with vegetables entries and HPB Singapore fried noodle data, adjusted for Schezwan sauce composition. Input record values are consistent with this adapted range and are retained. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for wheat-based stir-fried noodles. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this stir-fried noodle preparation is not available in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB Foodo database as of 2024. 2. water_g set to -1: moisture content for this prepared dish is not reported in the adapted source entries. 3. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: micronutrient data at this level of preparation detail is not available in the adapted approved sources. 4. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 (confirmed zero): this is a fully plant-based dish with no animal-derived ingredients; cholesterol is naturally zero. 5. The input record lists the restaurant category as Turkish/Kebab/Shawarma, which is a likely miscategorisation on the delivery platform; the dish itself is an Indo-Chinese preparation standard in Singapore. Cultural tags have been corrected accordingly. 6. Potassium estimated at 175 mg/100 g based on vegetable composition (cabbage, carrots, bell peppers, onions) using USDA SR Legacy component values; this is an estimate and should be verified if precise potassium data is required. 7. GI of 58 is an analogous estimate; direct GI measurement for this specific preparation has not been published as of 2024.

Added mandatory Singaporean multilingual names (Chinese simplified/traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi). Added health_context with all three required conditions including Type 2 Diabetes. Added GI estimate from University of Sydney GI Database analogous source. Added ontology anchors with honest gap notes. Expanded aliases and aliases_by_language. Documented all -1 fields in notes. Confirmed cholesterol 0.0 as naturally zero (plant-based dish). Verified aliases_by_language consistency with flat aliases array.
